Acute Myocardial Infarction (Heart Attac.Treatment: Emergency hospitalization, oxygen therapy, antiplatelet agents, anticoagulants, percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI), cardiac monitoring, ICU care

Stroke (Brain Attack).Treatment: Emergency CT/MRI, thrombolytic therapy (if ischemic within 4.5 hrs), antiplatelet therapy, blood pressure management, neurological monitoring, ICU care

Hypertensive Crisis.Treatment: IV antihypertensive medications, continuous BP monitoring, ICU care, gradual BP reduction, organ function assessment

Coronary Artery Disease (Acute).Treatment: Antiplatelet therapy, statins, beta-blockers, nitrates, possible angioplasty/stenting, cardiac rehabilitation

Heart Failure (Acute).Treatment: Oxygen therapy, diuretics, vasodilators, inotropic support if needed, fluid management, ICU monitoring

Pneumonia (Severe).Treatment: Hospitalization, IV antibiotics (broad-spectrum initially), oxygen therapy, respiratory support, hydration

Tuberculosis (Active).Treatment: Directly Observed Therapy (DOTS) with multi-drug regimen (rifampicin, isoniazid, pyrazinamide, ethambutol) for 6-9 months, isolation initially

Acute Respiratory Distress.Treatment: Emergency oxygen therapy, mechanical ventilation if needed, treat underlying cause, ICU care

Pulmonary Embolism.Treatment: Anticoagulation therapy (heparin/LMWH), thrombolytics if massive PE, oxygen therapy, ICU monitoring

Diabetic Ketoacidosis (DKA).Treatment: IV fluids, insulin therapy, electrolyte replacement, continuous monitoring, ICU care

Hyperosmolar Hyperglycemic State.Treatment: Aggressive IV fluid resuscitation, insulin therapy, electrolyte correction, ICU monitoring

Severe Hypoglycemia.Treatment: Immediate glucose administration (IV dextrose or glucagon injection), continuous monitoring, identify cause

Thyroid Storm.Treatment: Antithyroid medications, beta-blockers, corticosteroids, cooling measures, ICU care

Severe Anemia.Treatment: Blood transfusion, identify and treat underlying cause (iron, B12, folate deficiency or bleeding), hospitalization

Thrombocytopenia (Severe).Treatment: Platelet transfusion, treat underlying cause, bleeding precautions, hospitalization

Leukemia (Suspected).Treatment: Comprehensive blood work, bone marrow biopsy, referral to oncology center, chemotherapy if confirmed

Dengue Hemorrhagic Fever.Treatment: Hospitalization, IV fluid therapy, platelet monitoring, supportive care, no NSAIDs, strict monitoring

Sepsis.Treatment: ICU admission, broad-spectrum IV antibiotics, IV fluids, vasopressor support, source control, organ support

Malaria (Severe).Treatment: IV antimalarial therapy (artesunate), supportive care, ICU monitoring, blood transfusion if needed

Typhoid Fever (Severe).Treatment: IV antibiotics (ceftriaxone/azithromycin), IV fluids, supportive care, hospitalization

Meningitis.Treatment: Emergency IV antibiotics, steroids, lumbar puncture, ICU monitoring, isolation

COVID-19 (Severe).Treatment: Oxygen therapy, antivirals (remdesivir), steroids (dexamethasone), anticoagulation, respiratory support, ICU if needed

Acute Pancreatitis.Treatment: NPO (nothing by mouth), IV fluids, pain management, nutritional support, treat underlying cause, hospitalization

Peritonitis.Treatment: Emergency surgery, IV antibiotics, fluid resuscitation, ICU care

Gastrointestinal Bleeding (Severe).Treatment: Emergency endoscopy, blood transfusion, IV proton pump inhibitors, hemostatic procedures, ICU monitoring

Acute Kidney Injury.Treatment: IV fluids or fluid restriction, treat underlying cause, electrolyte management, possible dialysis

Chronic Kidney Disease (Advanced).Treatment: Blood pressure control, anemia management, phosphate binders, dietary restrictions, dialysis preparation

Brain Hemorrhage.Treatment: Emergency CT scan, blood pressure control, neurosurgical evaluation, ICU monitoring, possible surgery

Seizure Disorders (Status Epilepticus).Treatment: Benzodiazepines, anticonvulsants, airway protection, IV access, ICU monitoring, identify trigger

Encephalitis.Treatment: IV antivirals (acyclovir if HSV suspected), steroids, supportive care, ICU monitoring, lumbar puncture

Common Cold.Treatment: Rest, hydration, symptomatic relief (decongestants, antihistamines), warm fluids, steam inhalation

Seasonal Flu (Influenza).Treatment: Rest, hydration, antipyretics (paracetamol), antiviral medications if started early (oseltamivir), monitor for complications

Acute Bronchitis.Treatment: Rest, hydration, cough suppressants, bronchodilators if wheezing, usually viral (antibiotics only if bacterial)

Allergic Rhinitis.Treatment: Antihistamines, nasal corticosteroid sprays, allergen avoidance, saline nasal rinses

Sinusitis.Treatment: Saline irrigation, decongestants, nasal corticosteroids, pain relievers, antibiotics if bacterial (10-14 days)

Gastritis.Treatment: Proton pump inhibitors, H2 blockers, antacids, dietary modifications, avoid NSAIDs, H. pylori testing

Acid Reflux/GERD.Treatment: Proton pump inhibitors, lifestyle modifications (weight loss, avoid triggers), elevate head while sleeping, dietary changes

Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS).Treatment: Dietary modifications (low FODMAP), fiber supplements, antispasmodics, probiotics, stress management, psychological support

Gastroenteritis (Viral).Treatment: Oral rehydration solutions (ORS), rest, gradual diet reintroduction, anti-emetics if severe, monitor for dehydration

Indigestion.Treatment: Antacids, dietary modifications, smaller frequent meals, avoid trigger foods, over-the-counter medications

Food Poisoning (Mild).Treatment: Hydration with ORS, rest, avoid solid food initially, bland diet, probiotics, medical attention if severe/persistent

Type 2 Diabetes (Early/Managed).Treatment: Lifestyle modifications (diet, exercise), oral hypoglycemics (metformin first-line), blood sugar monitoring, HbA1c tracking

Pre-diabetes.Treatment: Intensive lifestyle modifications (weight loss 5-7%, exercise 150 min/week), dietary counseling, regular monitoring

Hyperlipidemia.Treatment: Statins, dietary modifications (low saturated fat), regular exercise, weight management, periodic lipid monitoring

Metabolic Syndrome.Treatment: Comprehensive lifestyle modification, medications for individual components, weight loss target, regular monitoring

Arthritis (Osteoarthritis).Treatment: Pain relievers (paracetamol, NSAIDs), physiotherapy, weight management, joint protection strategies, topical analgesics

Rheumatoid Arthritis (Early).Treatment: Disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs (DMARDs), NSAIDs, physiotherapy, regular monitoring

Muscle Strain.Treatment: Rest, ice/heat therapy, pain relievers (paracetamol, NSAIDs), gentle stretching, gradual return to activity

Back Pain (Non-specific).Treatment: Pain relievers, muscle relaxants, physiotherapy, heat therapy, exercise, posture correction

Fibromyalgia.Treatment: Pain management (duloxetine, pregabalin), sleep improvement, exercise, cognitive behavioral therapy, stress management

Upper Respiratory Tract Infection.Treatment: Rest, hydration, symptomatic relief (decongestants, antihistamines), warm gargles, steam inhalation

Pharyngitis.Treatment: Pain relievers, warm salt water gargles, throat lozenges, hydration, antibiotics only if bacterial (strep throat)

Tonsillitis.Treatment: Antibiotics if bacterial, pain relievers, antipyretics, rest, hydration, warm gargles

Urinary Tract Infection (Simple).Treatment: Antibiotics (nitrofurantoin, trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole) 3-7 days, increased fluid intake, cranberry supplements

Conjunctivitis.Treatment: Antibiotic eye drops if bacterial, antihistamine drops if allergic, warm compresses, hygiene measures

Hypertension (Stage 1).Treatment: Lifestyle modifications initially (DASH diet, exercise, weight loss, salt restriction), single antihypertensive if needed, regular monitoring

Prehypertension.Treatment: Intensive lifestyle modifications, weight loss, regular exercise, dietary changes, stress management, regular BP monitoring

Hypotension (Chronic).Treatment: Increase salt/fluid intake, compression stockings, small frequent meals, avoid sudden position changes, identify cause

Iron Deficiency Anemia (Mild).Treatment: Oral iron supplements (ferrous sulfate), vitamin C for absorption, dietary counseling (green leafy vegetables, jaggery), treat underlying cause

Vitamin B12 Deficiency Anemia.Treatment: Vitamin B12 supplements (oral or injections), dietary modifications, fortified foods, treat underlying absorption issues

Tension Headache.Treatment: Pain relievers (paracetamol, ibuprofen), stress management, adequate sleep, relaxation techniques, posture correction

Migraine.Treatment: Acute treatment (triptans, NSAIDs), preventive medications if frequent, trigger avoidance, lifestyle modifications

Cluster Headache.Treatment: Acute: oxygen therapy, triptans; Preventive: verapamil, lithium; lifestyle modifications

Vertigo (Benign Positional).Treatment: Vestibular exercises (Epley maneuver), anti-vertigo medications (betahistine), anti-emetics if needed

Skin Rash (Allergic).Treatment: Antihistamines, topical corticosteroids, allergen avoidance, cool compresses, moisturizers

Eczema (Atopic Dermatitis).Treatment: Emollients, topical corticosteroids, antihistamines, avoid triggers, moisturize regularly

Psoriasis.Treatment: Topical treatments (corticosteroids, vitamin D analogs), phototherapy, systemic medications if severe, stress management

Anxiety Disorder.Treatment: Cognitive behavioral therapy, relaxation techniques, SSRIs/SNRIs if moderate-severe, stress management, regular exercise

Depression (Mild-Moderate).Treatment: Psychotherapy (CBT), antidepressants (SSRIs) if moderate-severe, lifestyle modifications, social support

Stress-related Disorders.Treatment: Stress management techniques, counseling, relaxation exercises, lifestyle modifications, possibly anxiolytics short-term

Hypothyroidism.Treatment: Levothyroxine replacement, regular TSH monitoring, dose adjustments, lifelong therapy

Hyperthyroidism.Treatment: Antithyroid medications (methimazole), beta-blockers for symptoms, radioactive iodine or surgery for definitive treatment

Vitamin D Deficiency.Treatment: Vitamin D supplementation (60,000 IU weekly for 8 weeks then maintenance), calcium, sun exposure

Obesity.Treatment: Dietary modifications (calorie deficit), regular exercise (150+ min/week), behavioral therapy, medical management if needed

Dehydration.Treatment: Oral rehydration (ORS, water, electrolyte drinks), rest, IV fluids if severe, treat underlying cause

Sleep Disorders.Treatment: Sleep hygiene, cognitive behavioral therapy, treat underlying causes (OSA, anxiety), possibly short-term sleep aids

Chronic Fatigue Syndrome.Treatment: Graded exercise therapy, cognitive behavioral therapy, symptomatic management, treat comorbidities

Heat Exhaustion.Treatment: Move to cool place, hydration with ORS, cool compresses, rest, monitor for heat stroke

Dehydration (Mild).Treatment: Increased fluid intake (water, ORS, coconut water), rest, avoid sun exposure

Nutritional Deficiencies.Treatment: Targeted supplementation (iron, B12, vitamin D, etc.), dietary counseling, treat malabsorption if present
